title: eriogonum nudum var. murinum
intro: Eriogonum nudum var. murinum, also known as desert trumpet or woolly buckwheat, is a perennial wildflower plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum nudum var. murinum include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
